section_miscategorizations.text: She has also had end-stage renal disease and hypertension.
section_miscategorizations.reason: End-stage renal disease and hypertension are active diagnoses/problems rather than patient-reported symptoms; they fit better in the clinician's diagnostic impression, though their presence in HPI does not substantially mislead.

section_miscategorizations.text: She is vomiting and experiences pain in her stomach and burning when she eats.
section_miscategorizations.reason: These are the current primary symptoms prompting evaluation and would typically be summarized in the Chief Complaint; however, describing them in the HPI is still clinically understandable.

section_miscategorizations.text: She has end-stage renal disease and chronic kidney disease.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This appears to restate known past diagnoses rather than provide new diagnostic synthesis; it could be more appropriately framed as part of the past medical history within the HPI, but its presence in Assessment is not seriously misleading.

section_miscategorizations.text: Schedule physical therapy for shoulder strengthening and range of motion.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This plan item addresses shoulder issues that are unrelated to the renal-focused visit and not mentioned elsewhere in the note; its inclusion in the Plan section is misleading for this encounter's documented problems.
